summary: Machine policy for whether users can change diagnostic data opt-in settings in Settings. Microsoft documents the same device policy path, default, and numeric meanings that the app writes, and the local ADMX confirms the exact value name.

setting.name: Diagnostic Data Opt-In Settings UI
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can show controls in Settings that let a user change diagnostic data choices. This policy decides whether that Settings UI stays available.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable this UI on managed systems so users cannot change diagnostic data settings from the Settings app.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling the UI makes diagnostic policy more consistent, but it removes user control in Settings.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx in the System Policy CSP and DataCollection.admx maps it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx. Both the ADMX and the Microsoft Learn page document 0 and 1 explicitly.

allowed_values.label: Enable diagnostic data opt-in settings
allowed_values.meaning: End users can change the device diagnostic settings in the Settings app.

allowed_values.label: Disable diagnostic data opt-in settings
allowed_values.meaning: Diagnostic data settings are disabled in the Settings app and users cannot change them there.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx = 1, which matches the documented state that disables the Settings UI.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1803 and later systems exposing the policy

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the default value as 0.

windows_defaults.notes: By default, users can change the device diagnostic settings in Settings.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems where user control over diagnostics settings is acceptable
avoid_for: Managed corporate systems with fixed telemetry policy
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want users to keep access to diagnostic data settings.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want diagnostics choices locked down by policy.
tradeoffs: Users can change diagnostic settings in Settings.
The device has less centralized control over that UI.

recommended_profiles.label: Managed diagnostics UI
avoid_for: Personal systems where the owner expects to change diagnostics settings

states.rationale: Disable the diagnostic-data opt-in UI in Settings.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep diagnostic data choices under policy control instead of user control.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the Settings app to remain fully user-adjustable.
tradeoffs: Diagnostic-data settings are locked down in Settings.
Users lose a familiar control surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft System Policy CSP: ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the default value, allowed values 0 and 1, user-facing behavior, and Group Policy mapping for the diagnostic data opt-in settings UI policy.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft DataCollection.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says users can change diagnostic settings by default, while setting the policy to disable the opt-in UI removes that control from Settings.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx policy
evidence.summary: DataCollection.admx shows DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection. The mirrored enum lists 1 as disable diagnostic data opt-in settings and 0 as enable them, with EnabledValue=1 and DisabledValue=0. This is the exact policy UI gate for the telemetry opt-in settings surface.

decision.why: Microsoft documents ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx directly in the System Policy CSP, including the exact DataCollection policy key, default, and 0/1 semantics. The page uses the CSP control name while the local DataCollection.admx confirms the registry value name DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx, and the app writes that exact machine policy surface.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: This policy setting controls whether users can change the device diagnostic settings in settings. If you set this policy setting to Disable diagnostic data opt-in settings, the Settings app diagnostic data opt-in settings page is disabled. ... Default Value 0. Allowed values: 0 (Default) Enable diagnostic data opt-in settings. 1 Disable diagnostic data opt-in settings. Group policy mapping: Name ConfigureTelemetryOptInSettingsUx ... Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection.
validation_proof.notes: The System Policy CSP page explicitly documents the same policy behavior, key path, and 0/1 semantics. The exact registry value name is confirmed by the local DataCollection.admx file as DisableTelemetryOptInSettingsUx. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-datacollection-optin-ui-admx.
